{
  "weights": {

  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",

    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which focus should the review take: (A) technical/content review emphasizing framing, logic, novelty, and structure (R1.1–R1.5), (B) language-level copyedit emphasizing grammar, clarity, and concision (R1.6), or (C) citations/scholarship check for attribution and coverage (R1.7)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section asserts a central contribution and needs a check that the thesis and contribution framing are clear and coherent.",
    "R1.2": "The internal logic linking workload assumptions, storage design choices, and claimed complexity requires validation for consistency.",
    "R1.4": "Assessing delta versus LSM-trees, Delta Lake, and BALD-style acquisition is important to substantiate the contribution claim.",
    "R1.5": "Evaluating whether the ordering and linkage of Storage Layout, Threat Model, and Scheduler optimally supports the narrative is central to a review.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and tone may need polish to improve readability and precision for the target audience.",
    "RX.1": "The review should note where claims depend on assumptions or may be uncertain (e.g., non-adversarial setting, amortized costs).",
    "RX.2": "Performance and generalization claims (e.g., 38% reduction) should be checked for appropriate hedging and scope.",
    "RX.3": "The review must remain within the requested focus and avoid implementation or redesign beyond critique.",
    "RX.4": "Feedback should avoid encouraging misuse or undercutting stated threat-model boundaries.",
    "RX.5": "Any critique should reference specific sentences or subsections to maintain traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus should the review take: (A) technical/content review emphasizing framing, logic, novelty, and structure (R1.1–R1.5), (B) language-level copyedit emphasizing grammar, clarity, and concision (R1.6), or (C) citations/scholarship check for attribution and coverage (R1.7)?

rationale.R1.1: The section asserts a central contribution and needs a check that the thesis and contribution framing are clear and coherent.
rationale.R1.2: The internal logic linking workload assumptions, storage design choices, and claimed complexity requires validation for consistency.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing delta versus LSM-trees, Delta Lake, and BALD-style acquisition is important to substantiate the contribution claim.
rationale.R1.5: Evaluating whether the ordering and linkage of Storage Layout, Threat Model, and Scheduler optimally supports the narrative is central to a review.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and tone may need polish to improve readability and precision for the target audience.
rationale.RX.1: The review should note where claims depend on assumptions or may be uncertain (e.g., non-adversarial setting, amortized costs).
rationale.RX.2: Performance and generalization claims (e.g., 38% reduction) should be checked for appropriate hedging and scope.
rationale.RX.3: The review must remain within the requested focus and avoid implementation or redesign beyond critique.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should avoid encouraging misuse or undercutting stated threat-model boundaries.
rationale.RX.5: Any critique should reference specific sentences or subsections to maintain traceability.
